reasons: Khufra is a strong anti-dive answer because Helcurt must enter close range before finishing a target.
Khufra can protect backline allies by occupying the path Helcurt wants to use during Dark Night Falls.

proof.summary: Helcurt depends on blink entry and close-range burst, while Khufra can deny or punish direct dive paths before Helcurt reaches the backline.
worksBestWhen: Khufra stays near the vulnerable marksman or mage instead of chasing blindly during Helcurt's ultimate.
The team groups tightly enough for Khufra to cover the likely engage path.
failureCases: Helcurt waits until Khufra uses his anti-dive tool first.
Helcurt attacks from fog while Khufra is zoning elsewhere.

reasons: Hilda can pressure bushes and invade paths, making it harder for Helcurt to set up unseen pickoffs.
Her durability and early skirmish strength reduce Helcurt's comfort when he wants to snowball from isolated targets.

proof.summary: Helcurt gains value from unseen approach and darkness pressure; Hilda can contest side bushes and jungle entrances so Helcurt has fewer clean angles.
worksBestWhen: Hilda actively checks river and jungle entrances before objectives.
The team reacts to Hilda's scouting instead of splitting alone.
failureCases: Hilda checks vision alone without backup and becomes the target.
Helcurt avoids Hilda and attacks the opposite side.

reasons: Kaja can punish Helcurt after he commits into the backline.
Suppression-style lockdown is valuable against assassins that rely on fast burst windows.

proof.summary: Helcurt wants a short burst window after entering; Kaja can hold a direct lockdown answer for the moment Helcurt appears.
worksBestWhen: Kaja saves his lockdown for Helcurt instead of using it on the frontline.
Allies are close enough to burst Helcurt during the control window.
failureCases: Helcurt baits Kaja's engage tool before committing.
Kaja is forced to use his control on another diver.

reasons: Saber can punish Helcurt when Helcurt exits darkness or fails to instantly kill the target.
Point-target burst is useful against an assassin who must reveal himself to finish kills.

proof.summary: Helcurt is most dangerous during his engage and stinger burst window; Saber can answer after Helcurt commits and his escape path becomes predictable.
worksBestWhen: Saber waits for Helcurt to enter before committing.
Saber has backup damage to finish Helcurt quickly.
failureCases: Saber is the first target and dies before using his burst.
Helcurt enters only after Saber has used ultimate elsewhere.

reasons: Popol and Kupa can make flank routes harder for Helcurt by using traps and Kupa's zone presence.
This helps protect immobile backline heroes during Dark Night Falls.

proof.summary: Helcurt wants unseen flank access; Popol and Kupa can pre-place control around common entry paths so Helcurt has fewer clean approach angles.
worksBestWhen: Traps are placed before Helcurt casts ultimate.
Popol plays near the carry Helcurt wants to kill.
failureCases: Traps are placed too late after Helcurt has already entered.
Helcurt approaches from an unwarded side path.
